stories.title: The Brave Little Turtle
stories.setting: A green forest near a sparkling pond
stories.story: Tommy the Turtle was slow, but he was very brave. One day, Lily the Rabbit dropped her favorite ball into the deep pond. 'Oh no!' cried Lily. 'I cannot swim!' Sammy the Squirrel said, 'The water is too cold!' Tommy said, 'I am not fast, but I can swim. I will help you.' Tommy dove into the pond. He swam down, down, down. He found the ball and brought it back to Lily. 'Thank you, brave Tommy!' said Lily. 'You are slow on land, but you are fast in water!' Everyone cheered for the brave little turtle.
stories.moral: Everyone has special talents. You don't have to be fast to be brave.

stories.title: The Sharing Cloud
stories.setting: A bright blue sky above a garden
stories.story: Puffy was a little cloud who loved to rain. But Sunny the Sun said, 'Stop raining! You are blocking my sunshine!' Puffy felt sad. He wanted to help, but Sunny was angry. Daisy the Flower looked up and said, 'Please, Puffy, I am so thirsty! Rain on me!' Puffy smiled. He rained gently on Daisy. The flower grew tall and colorful. Sunny saw how happy Daisy was. 'I am sorry, Puffy. Rain is important too,' said Sunny. From that day, Sunny and Puffy worked together. Sunny shone, and Puffy rained. The garden was beautiful.
stories.moral: Working together is better than fighting.

stories.title: The Lost Mitten
stories.setting: A snowy winter day in a small town
stories.story: Emma lost her red mitten in the snow. 'Max, can you help me find it?' she asked her dog. Max sniffed the ground. He followed a trail through the park. They saw a squirrel playing with something red! 'That's my mitten!' said Emma. The squirrel ran up a tree. Emma said, 'Please, squirrel, I am cold.' The squirrel felt sorry for Emma. It dropped the mitten. Emma put it on her hand. 'Thank you, squirrel!' she said. Emma gave the squirrel a nut. 'Now we are friends,' she said.
stories.moral: Being kind and asking nicely can solve problems.

stories.title: The Listening Ears
stories.setting: A busy classroom with colorful posters
stories.story: Leo loved to talk. He talked during reading time, math time, and even during quiet time. His friend Mia could not hear the teacher. 'Leo, please be quiet,' said Mia. 'I cannot learn.' Leo did not listen. Then, Mr. Jenkins said, 'Leo, please stay after school.' Leo was sad. 'Why do I have to stay?' Mr. Jenkins said, 'Because your friends cannot hear. Listening is part of learning.' Leo practiced being quiet. He listened to his friends. He learned more that week than ever before. 'Thank you for teaching me to listen,' Leo said to Mr. Jenkins.
stories.moral: Listening is as important as talking.

stories.title: The Colorful Butterfly
stories.setting: A sunny meadow with many flowers
stories.story: Caterpillar Carl was sad because he was not beautiful. 'Look at Butterfly Bella,' he said. 'She has pretty wings. I am just a green worm.' Ant Tony said, 'Be patient, Carl. You will change.' Carl ate leaves and grew bigger. One day, he made a cocoon. He stayed inside for many days. When he came out, he had wings! Red, orange, and yellow wings! 'Look at me!' said Carl. 'I am a butterfly now!' Bella smiled. 'You were always special, Carl. You just needed time to grow.'
stories.moral: Everyone grows and changes. Be patient with yourself.
